name: Hubbsina turneri
summary: The Hubbsina turneri, a small fish found exclusively in permanent freshwater wetlands across Central America, is now critically endangered and facing a decreasing population trend. Its survival is threatened by habitat loss due to water abstraction and development, alongside recreational activities and increasing human presence.  You can help protect this unique fish by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible water management practices.

name: Ameca splendens
summary: The Ameca splendens, a stunning and critically endangered fish, is found only in the freshwater wetlands and rivers of central Mexico, including areas with springs and waterfalls. This unique species is facing severe threats from habitat destruction like dam construction and water diversion, along with agricultural and recreational pressures. You can help protect the Ameca splendens by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for sustainable water management practices in its native region.

name: Neochanna burrowsius

description.en: Artificial/Aquatic - Canals and Drainage Channels, Ditches

summary: The Neochanna burrowsius, a small, unique fish, is critically endangered and found in wetland habitats across Australia, including irrigation channels and rivers. Sadly, its survival is threatened by human activities like farming, water abstraction, and habitat modification. You can help by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland restoration and advocating for sustainable water management practices in these vital areas.

name: Allotoca diazi
summary: The Allotoca diazii, also known as the Desert Pupfish, is a critically endangered fish found exclusively in a handful of unique wetland habitats across Baja California, Mexico – specifically in permanent freshwater lakes, rivers, and springs. Sadly, this remarkable species faces severe threats from pollution, water abstraction for agriculture and domestic use, and habitat degradation.  You can help protect the Desert Pupfish by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible water management practices in the region.

name: Poblana alchichica
summary: The Poblana alchichica is a critically endangered fish species found exclusively in the wetlands and freshwater lakes of central Mexico. Sadly, its numbers are decreasing due to growing tourism, recreational activities, and the draining of groundwater for human use.  You can help protect this unique fish by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible water management practices.

name: Poblana ferdebueni
summary: The Poblana killifish, a critically endangered species, is found exclusively in freshwater wetlands across central Mexico, including seasonal lakes and rivers. Sadly, this unique fish is facing an unknown set of threats, compounded by pollution, habitat loss due to erosion and water diversion, and potentially competition with other species. You can help protect the Poblana killifish by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland restoration and advocating for sustainable water management practices in its fragile habitat.

name: Poblana letholepis
summary: The Poblana letholepis is a critically endangered fish species found exclusively in freshwater wetlands across central Mexico, including seasonal lakes, rivers, and streams. Sadly, this unique fish is facing severe threats from habitat loss due to soil erosion and water diversion for agriculture, alongside the impact of other unknown species.  You can help protect this amazing fish by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for sustainable water management practices in its native region.

name: Poblana squamata
summary: The Poblana Squamata, a unique fish species, is critically endangered and sadly declining, primarily found in the wetlands and rivers of central Mexico. These fish are facing unknown threats, likely exacerbated by the construction of dams and other developments impacting their freshwater habitats.  You can help protect this vulnerable species by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible water management practices in the region.

name: Clarias batu
summary: The Clarias batu, a unique catfish, is critically endangered and sadly declining in numbers. Found in the wetlands of permanent rivers, streams, and creeks across Southeast Asia, this fish is facing severe threats from water abstraction for agriculture and domestic use, as well as increasing pressure from tourism.  You can help protect the Clarias batu by supporting sustainable water management practices and reducing your impact on wetland habitats.

name: Oreoglanis lepturus
summary: The Oreoglanis lepturus is a critically endangered fish found exclusively in the unique wetlands of inland rivers and streams across Southeast Asia, particularly in areas with waterfalls. These incredible fish are facing severe threats from pollution like sewage and mining runoff, as well as habitat disruption from dams and human development. You can help protect this species by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible water management practices.

name: Betta fusca
summary: The Malayan trumpetfish, *Betta fusca*, is a critically endangered species found exclusively in the freshwater wetlands of Southeast Asia, particularly within permanent rivers, streams, and creeks – even those with waterfalls. Sadly, the exact threats to this unique fish remain unknown, and its future is uncertain.  You can help protect this vulnerable species by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and sustainable fisheries management in Southeast Asia.

name: Hyalobagrus ornatus
summary: The Hyalobagrus ornatus, also known as the "Chinese Paddlefoot Loach," is a critically endangered fish found exclusively in the wetlands of China, inhabiting permanent rivers, streams, and even waterfalls. Sadly, this unique species faces significant threats from expanding agriculture, urbanization, and the use of harmful chemicals in the surrounding areas.  You can help protect this vulnerable fish by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for sustainable land management practices.

name: Probarbus jullieni
summary: The Jullien’s barbell, a strikingly beautiful fish, is critically endangered and facing a decreasing population trend. These fish primarily live in freshwater wetlands and rivers throughout Central America, but are threatened by overfishing, both commercially and for local sustenance, as well as the construction of dams.  You can help protect the Jullien’s barbell by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for sustainable fishing practices in its habitat!

name: Pseudotocinclus juquiae
summary: The Jewel Tetra is a stunning, critically endangered fish native to the unique wetlands of the upper Rio Negro basin in Brazil, specifically found in permanent rivers, streams, and even waterfalls. Sadly, this tiny fish is facing severe threats from pollution like sewage discharge, habitat destruction due to expanding urban areas, and a lack of detailed information about its population. You can help protect the Jewel Tetra by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for sustainable water management practices in the Amazon region.

name: Notholebias fractifasciatus
summary: This critically endangered goby, found only in small, seasonal freshwater marshes across South America, is facing severe threats from habitat loss due to expanding urban areas and ecosystem modifications. Sadly, garbage and solid waste also contribute to the decline of this unique species. You can help by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and reducing your plastic consumption to protect these vital habitats!

name: Leptopanchax itanhaensis
summary: The Leptopanchax itanhaensis, or Itanha Emersed Fish, is a critically endangered species found exclusively in the freshwater wetlands and rivers of southeastern Brazil, particularly in subtropical and tropical rainforest areas. Sadly, this unique fish is facing severe threats from habitat loss due to development and unsustainable harvesting practices, both large-scale and small-scale.  You can help protect this vulnerable species by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible land-use planning in its native habitat.

name: Hypsolebias rufus

description.en: Wetlands (inland) - Bogs, Marshes, Swamps, Fens, Peatlands

summary: The *Hypsolebias rufus*, or Rufus Climbing Gar, is a tiny, critically endangered fish found exclusively in unique, seasonal wetlands across Central America, particularly in areas like Guatemala and Honduras. These incredible creatures face severe threats from habitat destruction due to roads, railroads, and other changes to their delicate marsh environments, and their populations are sadly declining. You can help protect this species by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible land-use planning in these vital ecosystems.

name: Listrura nematopteryx

description.en: Wetlands (inland) - Bogs, Marshes, Swamps, Fens, Peatlands

summary: The Listrura nematopteryx, also known as the Bog Doll, is a truly remarkable fish found exclusively in the unique wetlands of Europe – specifically in bogs, marshes, and peatlands. Sadly, this critically endangered species is threatened by the draining of these vital habitats for development and water abstraction, with the exact reasons for water loss still unclear.  You can help protect the Bog Doll by supporting organizations dedicated to wetland conservation and advocating for responsible water management practices.
